title: Desmos conchyliatus
intro: Desmos conchyliatus, commonly known as Conch Shell Bush, is a plant species native to Australia and New Caledonia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Desmos conchyliatus include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Conch Shell Bush is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Desmos conchyliatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Desmos. The classification of Desmos conchyliatus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Polypodiaceae, genus Desmos, species D. conchyliatus. This species is native to New Guinea and surrounding islands. It is also known as the Conch Shell Vane Fern.
